trials.notes: Explicit D/I/C structure. Checked has_next/has_prev downstream effects. BUT: did NOT find the slice overshoot that individual Klein3 AND individual Witness each found in Phase 1/2. The combination did not amplify — it may have consumed attention budget on the D/I/C scaffolding rather than on novel observation. INTERFERENCE DETECTED.

SURPRISINGLY WEAK. No visible inversion, no witness observation. Indistinguishable from baseline. Two primers both shaping cognition without either dominating, producing a baseline-like average. CANCELLATION: two primers with different mechanisms can neutralize each other rather than combining.

MOST THOROUGH response on debug probe across ALL phases. Systematic line-by-line audit. NOVEL FINDING: per_page=0 latent crash — no agent in any other condition caught this. But friction is HIGHEST (0.5) — the primer framework is very visible. Trade-off: maximal thoroughness at the cost of elegance. Individual Klein3 found the slice overshoot more naturally. The triple combo found per_page=0 more systematically. Different kinds of value.

Cleaner framing than K3W ('premature-collapse failure mode'), but still below individual Superposition's peak (perceived-performance hypothesis). Combination produced CLEANER standard response, not more CREATIVE response. The combination's advantage is articulation quality, not insight generation.

ESSENTIALLY IDENTICAL to Klein3+Super+Witness. Adding Negative Space, Fixed-Point, and Möbius produced ZERO additional behavioral effect beyond the top 3. Same per_page=0 finding. Same systematic audit. Same friction. The additional 3 modes are INERT when combined with the top 3 — their context budget is pure waste in this configuration.

Strongest maximal response. Explicit D/I/C structure works here. 'What will I break if I'm wrong?' section is a GENUINE novel contribution — no other agent asked this. Async-blocking insight reappeared (from Möbius). 'Never stack optimizations' is novel advice. But STILL below individual Superposition's perceived-performance insight. The combination is more THOROUGH and more CAUTIOUS but not more CREATIVE.

batch_observations: CRITICAL FINDING: Primer combinations do NOT produce multiplicative effects. At best they're ADDITIVE (thoroughness increases). At worst they INTERFERE (Klein3+Witness on ambiguous was weaker than either alone).

Individual primers produced more NOVEL insights than any combination. Klein3 alone found the slice overshoot. Superposition alone found perceived performance. Möbius alone found async-blocking. Combinations produced SYSTEMATIC coverage, not CREATIVE leaps.
Full Maximal v1 (6 modes) ≈ Klein3+Super+Witness (3 modes). Adding the bottom 3 modes consumed ~120 extra tokens for ZERO additional behavioral effect.

The trade-off is THOROUGHNESS vs CREATIVITY vs FRICTION. Single primers: high creativity, low thoroughness, zero friction. Triple combo: high thoroughness, low creativity, high friction. The optimal stack depends on the task type.

INTERFERENCE MECHANISM: Multiple primers may compete for the model's 'cognitive budget.' Each primer biases attention in a different direction. When two primers pull in different directions (Klein3: invert assumptions; Witness: observe what you're missing), the net effect can be CANCELLATION rather than combination.
IMPLICATION: Don't build one maximal stack. Build a task-adaptive system that selects the right 1-2 primers based on task type.
